data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Shopify, Citing Agentic AI Initiatives and Enterprise/B2B Momentum as Key Upside Drivers
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst John Shao maintained a Buy rating on Shopify yesterday and set a price target of $159.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

John Shao has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Shopify’s execution and emerging growth drivers. He anticipates first-quarter results to align with expectations despite seasonal softness, while emphasizing that clearer guidance on free cash flow margins and progress in enterprise and B2B segments could support a recovery in the valuation multiple.

Shao also highlights Shopify’s Agentic initiatives and AI tools as important, early-stage catalysts. He views the Agentic plan as a strategic way to capture additional GMV by acting as a neutral commerce channel, and sees Sidekick as a tool that can push merchants toward higher-tier subscriptions, lifting recurring revenue. Although monetization of Agentic commerce is still nascent, he expects more disclosure and momentum, which underpins his constructive stance on the shares.

In another report released on April 22, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $170.00 price target.

SHOP’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from C$245.170 to C$169.490, which is a -30.87% drop .
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Raiz Invest: Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Earnings Outlook Lifted and $1.05 Price Target Maintained on Resilient Growth Drivers
data.description:
Caleb Weng, an analyst from PAC Partners, maintained the Buy rating on Raiz Invest Ltd.. The associated price target is A$1.05.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Caleb Weng has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including upgraded forecasts for Raiz Invest’s FY26 revenue and EBITDA that now sit near the high end of company guidance, supported by solid active customer numbers and robust ARPU. He views the business model as resilient, noting that customer growth has remained strong and ARPU has held up despite recent market volatility, which underpins confidence in achieving the lifted earnings outlook.

He also highlights multiple growth catalysts, such as the planned introduction of direct ASX and U.S. trading, a new Raiz app, and increased marketing initiatives, all of which are expected to further boost customer acquisition, retention, and monetisation. In addition, the rebound in funds under management after a market-driven dip and the rising mix of higher-value premium customers support the view that Raiz is well positioned for continued growth, justifying the maintained $1.05 price target and Buy recommendation.
